action: note [Catso: I wanted these to listen to music while cutting grass with a riding and walk-behind mower.The ear cups MAY be ok for children (dint have any to test this on), but for adult sized ears they are way too small - as such, they do very little to cancel any nose. Also, because they are so small, they tend to not be very comfortable to use for extended periods.The sound quality is OK, I guess, nothing special here. What they do, they do OK, also they were fairly easy to pair (wouldn't pair with my S10+ the 1st time around, paired well with Tab 6, then paired easily with S10+ the 2nd time)I will be keeping them, but will be looking for a better pair for mowing]

action: note [Chris Moran: Really a 3.5. It was a pain to put on without any subscreen issues. I got the bubbles *mostly* out, but the tiniest speck of dust caused a noticeable blemish that annoyed me eventually to the point of removing it to put back on. I had to pick at the sticky side to get the dust off which left marks from my finger. I washed the sticky side in hopes that it was like some other stick/adhesives that are meant to be washed. It didn't seem to mees up the adhesive capability of the scree, but it doesn't look as night and didn't install super cleanly.I have the second screen, which is good to have, for when this one bugs me enough to replace. I guess one needs to be VERY careful about their environment when they install it. The very edges never REALLY adhered, but they aren't curling up or anything bad and they are just beyond he actual LCD area, so don't mess with how it looks.I've not put it through any stress tests and typically only have rare loose change in the same pocket, but it's not got a scratch on it after 3 months.]

instruction: List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about good fingerprint resistant
action: note [Andrew Sanders: This is my third purchase of this item. It has proven rugged, but the slide off the roof to the gravel driveway below was too much for it, and used up my last protector from my previous order.Had others in the past and they didn't seem to hold up as well. Additionally, I ALWAYS have an issue with them lifting up at the edges, probably because of the stresses induced when you "roll" them down from center to edge or from one edge to the other. This one seems to lift less than others I've used.If the phone survives another year of my abuse, I'll probably be reordering another set of these without having to spend hours researching which one is best.]

action: note [Rachel: It is super clear and fingerprint resistant. It was kind of hard trying to get it on, and I did get some hairs on the sticky side, but all in all it was great! Bubbles went away around the small hairs so you can barely tell they are there. They also give you tons of extra tools to help you clean the screen and get dust particles off of the screen before you put it on. I think it was just me being clumsy with all of the dust particles getting inside the screen.]

instruction: List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about good fingerprint resistant

action: note [T. Gannon: Nice product. Seems much better than the one replaced, it does seem to resist fingerprints and stay cleaner. This is noticeable especially for swipe entries as my finger glides much more easily in the screen. All else is good so far. I didn't do a great job putting it on, a few bubbles and dust specks. But it works so well I don't want to remove it.Not enough to remove a star, but the packaging is a bit overkill. I think it can be recycled but the cost could come down even more without the tin case.]

instruction: List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about good fingerprint resistant
action: note [Open Source Prof: The protector is high quality and relatively easy to install, with caveats. It needs to be about 1mm wider to fully cover the screen though - there's no "wiggle room" in placing the protector on the screen. If you have weak vision or shaky hands, you'll find this very frustrating.Speaking of installation, be sure to apply the hinges lengthwise on the phone as shown in online videos, not widthwise as shown in the instructions - the temporary hinge will be more stable. Also, the hinge adhesive isn't very strong - be sure to secure it well before proceeding with the installation procedure.Once installed, you'll really notice the improvement of glass over plastic protectors. Elimination of air bubbles is easy, the screen is brighter/clearer, and your finger just glides over the screen - nice!]

action: note [GoldfishGoldfish: I've had this printer about a year. It gives great print quality as long as you use the premium paper settings (doesn't actually have to be the premium paper) and set it to high quality. Printing standard quality on plain paper is really only good enough for printing documents, online tickets, things like that.I make & sell stickers, greeting cards, etc, and I've used this printer for that since my WF 7620 had a printhead go bad - which cost nearly as much to replace as the printer itself. The 3620 has done well, but like all printers, they now have designed obsolescence so they all have issues eventually. Recently it started being unable to align the printheads even after running numerous alignments and the colors are no longer coming out correctly. I started having those color issues about 8 months in but I was able to work around it for a while by adjusting the color settings, now no amount of adjustments corrects it. Tons of head cleans haven't fixed the issue so I'm replacing it with an EcoTank model - because this printer goes through ink cartridges like crazy. I do print a lot, probably 20 or more pages a week on average, and I buy cartridges about every month or two which gets really expensive and ends up being several hundred dollars a year in cartridges.Horrible ink cost (but kind of typical)I had no issues setting up the wifi - works great.The scan feature works great, I love that it can scan straight to PDF for documents.I've never used any devices other than my PC to print from it so I can't comment on print anywhere featuresI have no issues with the menu or buttonsI have had paper jam issues, where the paper doesn't actually jam, it just doesn't want to pull a sheet out of the tray. Usually if I open/close the tray and tell it to try again it resolves the issue.I had issues with my WF 7620 denting the end of the paper when pulling it from the tray and causing jams - this printer did not have that issue.This printer has never sat unused for more than a couple of days, so I have never had any issues with ink drying out.Print speed for plain documents is really fast, print speed for high quality photo type prints is much slower but I found it to be as expected for any printer laying down that much ink and detail.This printer is a good size, not hard to move around and not terribly heavy. One person can do it. The WF 7620 I had previously has a large scanner bed so that printer is a nightmare to move compared to this printer - it can be moved by one person but I don't recommend it if you like your back the way it is.I have printed on 110 lb card stock from the paper tray with little to no feed issues (other than the one feed issue that happens with any paper I put in)I have printed glossy sticker paper with the only issue being the leading edge of the image sometimes smudges but only if there's a lot of dark ink at the top of the page.Plain paper printed with no issuesGlossy holographic card stock printed with no issues.The only paper I have had consistently jam in this printer is Silhouette's sticker paper - no idea why, as it isn't any thicker than my glossy sticker paper from LDAll in all, for what I originally paid for it, it has done its job and served me well. I've worked it hard, and it held up to a lot of abuse.Attached pictures are items that were printed on this printer, greeting card, stickers, bookmarker (laminated after printing on matte cardstock)]

instruction: List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about average print quality
action: note [J. B. Shirey II: I tried using an Epson printer years ago. My first computer printer was an Epson, in fact. I didn't have much luck with it then, but tried Epson again after learning that they are cheap to operate due to the cheap cost of their inkjet cartridges. Unfortunately, it seems that Epson printers clog up and cannot be unclogged using the printer's normal functions. Some claim you can remove part of the printing mechanism and clean it manually by soaking it, but honestly, I don't care to take the chance on damaging the machine. This might be a good choice for someone who prints every day, especially since it can be operated more cheaply than most printers, but it isn't for me. I have finally given up on the Epson and have purchased another all-in-one system. The scanner, at least, works well and is fast.]

instruction: List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about average print quality
List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about average print quality
action: note [Roxanne Brandon Coffey: When it worked this printer was an OK printer. The print quality was average. Using the tools to improve the print quality (cleaning / alignment) were more of a waste of ink. It slightly improved for maybe 10 copies. Overall the printer was an ink hog. It would warn you that you were running low on ink, if you replaced the cartridge, when it cycled it would seemingly check all the ink colors thus resulting in other colors running low. I say this because it would show the black as low and the others as fine then I'd replace the black and suddenly magenta was low.Epson does not have driver updates for this. I get regular notices that my driver is out of date but the reality is that they don't support it any longer. This actually happened less than a year after buying it. Now when I try to use the printer or the scanner it prints the first inch and gives an error code. The suggested work around is to cancel all print jobs, turn off the printer and then try again. When I do that it may work for the first couple of pages of a document or a few copies of a document but will not do more than 5 pages at a time. I plan to continue to use it this way until the ink I have is gone. At that time I will be going to a second story balcony to drop it on to concrete.... unless I decide that is too cruel to the concrete.]

instruction: List out reviewers, if exist, who mention about average print quality
action: note [Bob in Vegas: Since the day my printer was purchased the color printing has not worked. I bought it NEW only a few months ago and I've tried everything. I thought it was the ink it came with originally, so I switched it out for new ink and it still does a poor job(see the attached pictures). I did A LOT of research for home-office printers and actually this one came very highly rated! I read reviews, I did the consumer reports research, I did it all! However, since the day I bought mine, it hasn't functioned properly! Now I am rating it with 1 star really for the COLOR PRINTING ONLY. I mean, who buys a color printer that can't print in color anyway and expects it to get over 1 star?I went online looking for a customer support phone number; I couldn't find one. So I sent a facebook message to Epson explaining the issue. I needed help quickly and I was hoping they could do it online since most of my experience with technical support for computer stuff has been bad and I'm stressed enough. Well, needless to say I think I've had my intelligence insulted by Epson more times than I can handle. The person basically told me a list of things I should try to AVOID with my Epson printer; I never knew a lot of these things. Apparently their printer works better if you download what you need to print, save it first to your computer then print it-- instead of printing directly off the screen or website.However, the worst was when the person on the phone with customer service (I wanted to avoid this) asked me to take out the ink cartridges and read her the numbers on it (making sure I had installed the right ink to begin with) and then CHECK THE BOTTOM FOR TAPE. I never said it wasn't printing ANY color by the way, I have been saying it prints color POORLY!! So by the end of my conversation she decided I'm eligible for a replacement under warranty. However, my printer arrived broken. They want to give me a refurbished one, which I politely explained angered me for a number of reasons. First, my printer still is brand new, and I didn't buy a used printer off Amazon. Second, it arrived this way, it isn't like it all the sudden broke, it's been broken the whole time. I really loved what Epson said though. Her technical support is CLEARLY not customer support because her reasoning behind me getting a refurbished one is laughable, "it's not like we can resell yours, it's defective". Well duh! However, what about the NEW ONE that I was promised when I paid money for it? That one I'm still kind of waiting for... Well my point is, BUYER BEWARE...DON'T BUY THIS PRINTER... DON'T DO IT, DON'T DO IT!]

action: note [AMAZON SHOPAHOLIC AMAZON SHOPAHOLIC: I bought this thing because it was on the recommended list by The Wirecutter, and it was 4+ stars here on Amazon at the time.I'm sure I've had worse printers, but I can't remember them. I bought this thing three years ago. I've always had print quality issues with it. Never used aftermarket, always OEM ink. Always fighting with the stupid print quality and lines. Heck, I probably burned through an entire set of cartridges just printing test pages and alignment pages. It seems to burn through the ink faster than it should when it would work. When I'm VPN'ed into another network for whatever reason it breaks printing on this guy until I disconnect and then the queued job will go through. Other printers don't have this issue.I'm about ready to take a sledgehammer to it right now because I'm trying to get the driver installed on my second laptop just so I can scan some invoices to email to a client and I've been fighting with the stupid installer for an hour now. First it detects the printer, but then can't "contact" it. I finally realize it's probably my VPN. Get off of that and now it detects just fine, but during the install wants to update the firmware. Fine. Only it fails for whatever reason. Then it just won't continue finishing installing the printer and scan driver.I will never ever shop an Epson again. This thing is such a piece of $#!7...]

action: note [Bob in Vegas: There's a reason ONE THIRD OF THE REVIEWS ON THIS PRINTER ARE ONE STAR. THERE'S A REASON....ACTUALLY, SEVERAL REASONS. Overall, it's a poorly designed piece of junk that does not perform reliably. It's consistent, but unfortunately consistently lousy .Changing ink cartridges successfully has been a constant challenge. It won't recognize replacement ink cartridges..Epson or clone...about half the time. When working in a home office without other printers to turn to, that alone is reason to take a sledge hammer to this piece of junk.The default setting is for two sided printing. I couldn't find how to change the default. Called customer service, and through very broken English was told there was no way to change their arbitrary 2-sided default other than to manually uncheck 2-sided printing in the printer dialogue box MANUALLY FOR EVERY SINGLE DOCUMENT YOU WANT TO PRINT. I found it hard to believe such a moronic process was the only option, so the following week I called back again with the same questions....how to set the default to one sided printing and was told the same answer I got on the prior call....in even harder to understand attempts at English language.I should have sent this loser back the first time it wouldn't recognize an Epson cartridge, which was the very first time a cartridge needed replacement....and almost every cartridge change since then. In addition to the recognition problem, the gizmo that holds the ink cartridges is supposed to slide to the left when the machine is opened up, but it sometimes does and sometimes doesn't. If it doesn't, it's very difficult to raise the lid over the cartridges as it's blocked by another part of the printer. Did I mention lousy design??Bottom line: steer clear of anything Epson. Their poor excuse for customer service is only exceeded by the terrible design and poor quality of their products.]
